content.value: While 個 (go3) is universal, Cantonese has specific classifiers for different shapes and categories. Using the correct one makes your Cantonese sound much more natural.

content.translation: Classifier for animals (cats, dogs, birds, etc.)

content.value: You already know 嘅 (ge3) for possession. However, when talking about a specific noun, you can often drop 'ge3' and use the Classifier instead!

content.translation: My cat (more natural than 'ngo5 ge3 maau1')

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: ni1 bun2 hai6 m4 hai6 nei5 ge3 syu1 aa3?

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: hai6 aa3. ni1 bun2 hai6 ngo5 bun2 syu1.

pages.title: Chapter 4 Complete!
pages.summary: You've mastered the core grammar of Cantonese: Classifiers! You can now count, point, and describe possessions naturally.
